summary: "Add Offer" is entirely client-side and non-persistent — no backend write of any kind occurs. The API surface for /offers exposes only GET (confirmed in apps/api/app/routers/offers.py and apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py — no POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE route is registered anywhere for offers). The frontend's AddOfferModal.onAdd() only appends to local React state (apps/web/src/app/dashboard/offers/page.tsx: `setAdded((prev) => [...prev, offer])`); it never calls fetch/apiRequest. A user who adds one or more real offers, then reloads the page, navigates to any other route and back, or even just browses away in-app, silently loses every added offer with zero warning, zero error, zero confirmation dialog. The button, the form, the success-looking 'New' badge and computed 'Total comp' all present as if the action succeeded and was saved.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 at https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/login
2. Navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/offers (empty state, 0 offers)
3. Click 'Add Offer', fill Company='MV-offer-Acme', Base=200000, Bonus=20000, Equity=30000, Location='Sydney · Hybrid', click 'Add offer'
4. Observe: modal closes, a new offer card 'MV-offer-Acme' with a 'New' badge, Total comp $250k, Fit score 'Pending' appears in the grid — looks fully successful
5. Open browser devtools Network tab (or capture via Playwright) — confirm NO POST/PUT request was fired to any endpoint during step 3 (only the original GET /api/workspaces/offers from page load)
6. Reload the page (F5) — the MV-offer-Acme card is gone; the screen reverts to the empty state
7. Directly call GET https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/api/workspaces/offers with the admin bearer token — response shows "offers": [] confirming nothing was ever created server-side

8. Alternatively: add an offer, then use in-app navigation (click 'Dashboard' in sidebar, then browser Back) instead of a hard reload — the offer is lost exactly the same way, proving it is pure ephemeral component state, not even a client-side cache
expected: Adding an offer is the core CRUD action of an 'Offer Comparison' screen; a paying user would reasonably expect it to be saved to their account and to still be there next time they open the app (as the wireframe's own populated state — Canva/Atlassian/ANZ — implies: durable, named, real offers). At minimum, a client-only/draft implementation should say so honestly (e.g. 'not saved yet' banner) rather than presenting a full success UI.
observed: The offer is only ever held in transient React component state. No endpoint exists to persist it. Reload, back/forward navigation, or simply leaving the tab all silently destroy the data with no warning to the user.

summary: Negotiation Coach's 'Suggested counter' and the generated 'Draft counter email' are broken/nonsensical: they always show '$0 base' and instruct the user to email a hiring manager asking to discuss 'a base of $0'. Root cause: the backend (both GET /workspaces/offers and GET /offers) hardcodes `negotiation.suggestedCounter: None` unconditionally (apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py:614, apps/api/app/routers/offers.py equivalent) — it is never computed from any real offer data, regardless of how many offers exist. The frontend's NegotiationCoach.tsx coalesces the null to 0 (`negotiation?.suggestedCounter ?? 0`) and renders it directly into both the summary line and a full pre-filled email draft as if it were a real, considered number.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/offers
2. Add at least one offer via 'Add Offer' (e.g. MV-offer-Acme, base 200000) so the Negotiation Coach panel renders (it is only shown when offers.length > 0)
3. Observe the panel: 'Suggested counter: $0 base'
4. Click 'Draft counter email'
5. Observe the generated email text: '...I'd like to discuss a base of $0. I'm confident the AI delivery experience I bring maps directly to the roadmap we discussed...'
6. Confirm via API: curl GET /api/workspaces/offers (or /api/offers) with a valid bearer token — response always contains "negotiation":{"suggestedCounter":null,...} regardless of offers present
7. Repeated in a second, fully fresh browser session — identical '$0 base' result
expected: A 'Negotiation Coach' feature marketed with copy like 'get negotiation coaching tailored to your priorities' (empty-state) should either compute a real, sane counter figure from the user's actual offer data, or — if that capability isn't implemented — omit the counter/email-draft feature entirely rather than emit a specific-sounding but false number that a user could copy-paste into a real negotiation email.
observed: suggestedCounter is unconditionally null server-side; the UI renders it as a literal '$0' in both the summary chip and the auto-generated draft email, in every account state, real or empty.
evidence: screens/offer-comparison/test-artifacts/run2__results.json (weights_and_negotiation_panels — suggestedCounter absent from initial fetch too)

summary: The Add-Offer modal's full-screen backdrop does not actually cover the full viewport: a full-width strip roughly 24 CSS px tall at the very top of the screen is left uncovered, even though the wrapper div is `fixed inset-0 z-50` with computed style top:0/right:0/bottom:0/left:0 (getBoundingClientRect nonetheless reports top:24, height:696 of a 720px-tall viewport — a rendering/hit-test discrepancy, not merely a CSS authoring mistake, since the applied inset is genuinely 0 by computed style). Background elements in that strip (the sidebar's top row and, critically, live Topbar controls including the Notifications link) remain both visible (undimmed in a full-page screenshot) and clickable while the modal is open with aria-modal="true". Clicking the Notifications bell's top edge while the Add-Offer modal is open, mid-draft, navigates the whole page away to /dashboard/approvals — silently destroying the in-progress modal and any unsaved form input, with no confirmation.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/offers
2. Click 'Add Offer' to open the modal; type something into the Company field (e.g. 'MV-offer-unsaved-work-probe')
3. Via devtools console (or Playwright): document.elementFromPoint(5,5) — returns the sidebar <aside> element, not the modal backdrop <div>
4. document.elementFromPoint(1074,13) (over the Notifications bell's top edge) — returns the notifications <a> link itself, aria-label 'Notifications — 5 pending approvals'
5. Click at those coordinates (mouse.click(1074, 13)) — the browser navigates to /dashboard/approvals; the Add-Offer modal and its draft text are gone with no warning

6. Take a full-page screenshot with the modal open — visually confirm the topbar/header band ('Good afternoon, Administrator...') is NOT dimmed by the black/blur overlay that darkens the rest of the page
7. Reproduced identically across two separate fresh browser sessions (run2 automated pass) plus 5 additional independent investigative sessions — 100% consistent
expected: An aria-modal="true" dialog with a full-screen backdrop should make the entire rest of the page visually inert and non-interactive — no gap, no click-through, no accidental navigation destroying in-progress form input. This is the same class of issue previously tracked as GAP-P4-057 (tab-order leak), except this is a pointer/hit-test leak in the same modal.
observed: A reproducible ~24px full-width band at the very top of the viewport is excluded from the modal's effective overlay coverage; real topbar controls there remain clickable and can navigate the user away, discarding the modal and any unsaved draft.
evidence: screens/offer-comparison/test-artifacts/investigate-modal-overlay-gap-visual.png (full-page screenshot showing undimmed top band while modal is open)
screens/offer-comparison/test-artifacts/scripts/backdrop-investigate4.cjs, backdrop-investigate6.cjs, backdrop-investigate9.cjs, backdrop-investigate10.cjs (isolation scripts + console output)
apps/web/src/components/offers/AddOfferModal.tsx:143-151 (fixed inset-0 z-50 wrapper + absolute inset-0 backdrop)

summary: The screen's own subtitle ('Weighted decision analysis across your live offers') and the 'Priority Weights' panel imply an interactive, computed ranking — but the weights (Compensation 30% / Growth 25% / Culture 20% / Location & flexibility 15% / Company stability 10%) are entirely static: there is no input control to adjust them (PriorityWeights.tsx renders read-only progress bars only), and no code anywhere computes a weighted score from them. The only per-offer number shown ('Fit score') is either the pre-existing, unrelated Job.fitScore field from job-matching (for real backend-sourced offers) or simply 'Pending' (for any offer added via the Add-Offer modal) — it is never derived from, or affected by, the Priority Weights panel in any way. Changing what you 'care about' has literally no effect anywhere on the screen because there is no mechanism to change it at all.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/offers
2. Read the page subtitle: 'Weighted decision analysis across your live offers'
3. Inspect the Priority Weights panel — Compensation/Growth/Culture/Location/Stability bars with fixed percentages, no sliders, no inputs, no drag handles, no edit affordance of any kind
4. Inspect apps/web/src/components/offers/PriorityWeights.tsx — confirm no onChange/interactive elements exist
5. Inspect apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py:602-608 — confirm the 5 weight rows are hardcoded literals returned on every request, not read from or written by any user preference
6. Confirm Fit score per offer card is independent of the weights: real offers show Job.fitScore verbatim; client-added offers always show 'Pending' regardless of the (immutable) weights
expected: A 'weighted decision analysis' feature should let the user express what they actually prioritize and see the ranking/fit-score respond to it — or, if that's not implemented yet, should not present a specific numeric breakdown (30/25/20/15/10) as though it is live and meaningful.
observed: Weights are decorative and fixed; no scoring logic anywhere consumes them.

summary: There is no way to remove or edit an offer once added — no delete/remove icon or edit affordance exists anywhere on an offer card, in either the wireframe or the shipped UI (they agree with each other here, so this is not a wireframe deviation, only a product-completeness gap). Combined with MV-offer-comparison-001 (no persistence), a user who mistypes a company name or salary during Add Offer has no in-product way to correct or remove just that one entry — full reload wipes everything, not just the bad entry.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/offers
2. Add an offer with a typo, e.g. Company='MV-offer-Acme' but meant to type a different name
3. Inspect the resulting offer card (apps/web/src/components/offers/OfferCard.tsx) — no delete/edit button, icon, or menu is rendered anywhere on the card

4. Confirm the wireframe (design/screens/offer-comparison.html, offer-card-of07/08/09) also has no such control — so this is a coverage gap against reasonable product expectations, not a build-vs-wireframe defect
expected: A comparison tool would reasonably let a user remove or correct a single entry without losing the rest of their work.
observed: No edit/delete UI exists on offer cards.

summary: All dollar figures on the screen are shown with a bare '$' and no currency code (e.g. '$250k'), even though the backend computes a currency-qualified `salaryRange` field (e.g. 'AUD 185,000–220,000') per real offer — the frontend Offer type/OfferCard never reads or renders `salaryRange` at all, so the computed field is dead weight and the UI never discloses which currency the numbers are in. Not a fabricated-math issue (no tax/take-home calculation is present on this screen to verify against AUD/GST reality — confirmed absent from both the wireframe and the implementation), just an omitted disambiguation on an Australia-focused platform where offers could plausibly be in AUD, USD, NZD, etc.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/offers, add any offer
2. Observe the offer card shows only '$NNNk' style figures with no currency code anywhere on the card

3. Inspect apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py:591-593 — `salaryRange` is computed with a currency prefix (defaulting 'AUD') but apps/web/src/lib/api/workspaces.ts's Offer type and OfferCard.tsx never consume/render it
expected: Given the computed backend field exists specifically to disambiguate currency, the UI should surface it, or otherwise label the currency once at the screen level.
observed: salaryRange (with currency code) is computed server-side but never displayed.
